summary: iOS | Morpheus | Unable to track inCall status
scenario: iOS app fails to detect active call state on Morpheus devices due to missing deckardId 0x0E35 support, requiring fallback to deckardId 0x0E22 for correct in-call status, DFU blocking, and ANC transparency control.
description_clean: The iOS application cannot reliably determine if a user is in a call when connected to Morpheus devices. This prevents the app from showing appropriate alerts or blocking DFU initiation and ANC transparency mode switching during active calls. Root cause: Morpheus devices do not send or respond to deckardId 0x0E35. The fix is to also use deckardId 0x0E22, which correctly reflects call state on Morpheus devices. Acceptance criteria: all devices correctly respond to in-call status; DFU availability is enabled/blocked based on call state; ANC transparency control is enabled/disabled accordingly.
steps: Connect iOS app to a Morpheus device
Initiate a phone call on the iOS device
Observe the app's ability to detect the in-call state
Attempt to initiate DFU (Device Firmware Update) while in a call
Attempt to switch ANC transparency mode while in a call
Verify that the app shows appropriate alerts or blocks these actions during the active call
expected: The app correctly detects the in-call state via deckardId 0x0E22, shows appropriate alerts, blocks DFU initiation, and disables ANC transparency mode switching during active calls on Morpheus devices.
actual: The app cannot reliably detect the in-call state because Morpheus devices do not send or respond to deckardId 0x0E35. DFU initiation and ANC transparency mode switching are not blocked during active calls.
